sections.paragraph:
περιτρέχω impf. περιέτρεχον; 2 aor. περιέδραμον, ptc. περιδραμών (Hom. et al.).

① to be in motion around, run/move around w. the acc. of the thing or pers. one moves around (Hdt. 8, 128; Aristoph., Ran. 193 τὴν λίμνην κύκλῳ; Athen. 5, 208b; PFlor 120, 7; GrBar 6:2) ἄγγελοι περιέτρεχον αὐτοὺς ἐκεῖσε angels were moving about them there ApcPt 5:18. ὧδε κἀκεῖσε περιτρ. κύκλῳ τῆς πύλης run here and there around the gate Hs 9, 3, 1.

② to be in rapid motion here and there in an area, run about, go about in (Cebes 14, 1; Am 8:12; Jer 5:1 ἐν ταῖς ὁδοῖς) w. acc. (Aristoph., Thesmoph. 657 τὴν πύκνα πᾶσαν) ὅλην τὴν χώραν Mk 6:55. ἐν τοῖς πρόβασι (v.l. προβάτοις) π. run about among the sheep Hs 6, 1, 6.—M-M.

impf. impf. = imperfect

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

acc. acc. = accusative

pers. pers. = person(s)

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

ApcPt ApcPt = Apocalypse of Peter—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Cebes Cebes , prominent in Platonic dialogues; ref. is to a dialogue associated with his name, I a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 807
